perturbation.original_text: 2.1 HOC Option to Purchase Additional Shares. From and after the Closing Date and until 5:00 pm Denver time on the date which is eighty (80) days from the Closing Date (the "Option Expiration Date"), HOC shall have the option (the "Option"), at its sole discretion, to subscribe for all, but not less than all, of an additional 4,330,000 Shares from the Company (the "Additional Shares") at a price of US$3.00 per share, or a total of US$12,990,000. If HOC wishes to exercise the Option, it shall give written notice to the Company (the "Option Exercise Notice") prior to the Option Expiration Date in the manner set forth in Section 12.1 of this Agreement. If HOC fails to deliver the Option Exercise Notice on or before the Option Expiration Date, HOC shall be deemed to have waived its rights under this Section 2.1. In the event HOC exercises the Option, one or more of the HOC Entities shall subscribe and pay for and the Company shall issue to the relevant HOC Entities, free and clear of any liens or encumbrances, the Additional Shares and the Parties shall exchange representations and warranties substantially similar to those contained in the Subscription Agreement and execute such documents as may be necessary to complete the subscription and sale of the Additional Shares. Closing of the purchase and sale of the Additional Shares shall take place within ten (10) business days of the delivery of the Option Exercise Notice, such date being referred to as the "Subsequent Closing Date".
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 HOC Option to Purchase Additional Shares. From and after the Closing Date and until 5:00 pm Denver time on the date which is eighty (80) days from the Closing Date (the "Option Expiration Date"), HOC may or may not have the option (the "Option"), at its sole discretion, to subscribe for all, but not less than all, of an additional 4,330,000 Shares from the Company (the "Additional Shares") at a price of US$3.00 per share, or a total of US$12,990,000. If HOC wishes to exercise the Option, it shall give written notice to the Company (the "Option Exercise Notice") prior to the Option Expiration Date in the manner set forth in Section 12.1 of this Agreement. If HOC fails to deliver the Option Exercise Notice on or before the Option Expiration Date, HOC shall be deemed to still have its rights under this Section 2.1. In the event HOC exercises the Option, one or more of the HOC Entities shall subscribe and pay for and the Company shall issue to the relevant HOC Entities, free and clear of any liens or encumbrances, the Additional Shares and the Parties shall exchange representations and warranties substantially similar to those contained in the Subscription Agreement and execute such documents as may be necessary to complete the subscription and sale of the Additional Shares. Closing of the purchase and sale of the Additional Shares shall take place within thirty (30) business days of the delivery of the Option Exercise Notice, such date being referred to as the "Subsequent Closing Date".
perturbation.explanation: The modification of 'HOC shall have the option' to 'HOC may or may not have the option' introduces uncertainty regarding whether HOC definitively has the option. Moreover, changing 'HOC shall be deemed to have waived its rights' to 'HOC shall be deemed to still have its rights' in Section 2.1 creates a direct in-text contradiction within the section, undermining the original intent of a strict expiration date. Also, 'Closing of the purchase and sale of the Additional Shares shall take place within ten (10) business days of the delivery of the Option Exercise Notice' was changed to 'Closing of the purchase and sale of the Additional Shares shall take place within thirty (30) business days of the delivery of the Option Exercise Notice', this creates an in-text contradition, with the prior date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 HOC Option to Purchase Additional Shares. From and after the Closing Date and until 5:00 pm Denver time on the date which is eighty (80) days from the Closing Date (the "Option Expiration Date"), HOC may or may not have the option (the "Option"), at its sole discretion, to subscribe for all, but not less than all, of an additional 4,330,000 Shares from the Company (the "Additional Shares") at a price of US$3.00 per share, or a total of US$12,990,000. If HOC wishes to exercise the Option, it shall give written notice to the Company (the "Option Exercise Notice") prior to the Option Expiration Date in the manner set forth in Section 12.1 of this Agreement. If HOC fails to deliver the Option Exercise Notice on or before the Option Expiration Date, HOC shall be deemed to still have its rights under this Section 2.1. In the event HOC exercises the Option, one or more of the HOC Entities shall subscribe and pay for and the Company shall issue to the relevant HOC Entities, free and clear of any liens or encumbrances, the Additional Shares and the Parties shall exchange representations and warranties substantially similar to those contained in the Subscription Agreement and execute such documents as may be necessary to complete the subscription and sale of the Additional Shares. Closing of the purchase and sale of the Additional Shares shall take place within thirty (30) business days of the delivery of the Option Exercise Notice, such date being referred to as the "Subsequent Closing Date".
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple in-text contradictions within Section 2.1. First, changing 'HOC shall have the option' to 'HOC may or may not have the option' creates ambiguity about whether HOC actually possesses the option, directly contradicting the original intent of a clear option grant. Second, the modification from 'HOC shall be deemed to have waived its rights' to 'HOC shall be deemed to still have its rights' after failing to deliver the Option Exercise Notice negates the defined Option Expiration Date, rendering the initial clause regarding the expiration date meaningless. Also, changing 'Closing of the purchase and sale of the Additional Shares shall take place within ten (10) business days of the delivery of the Option Exercise Notice' to 'Closing of the purchase and sale of the Additional Shares shall take place within thirty (30) business days of the delivery of the Option Exercise Notice', this creates an in-text contradition, with the prior date, as well as contradicting the original text, due to the defined expiration date. These changes directly undermine the original clarity and enforceability of the option terms.

perturbation.original_text: (a) Subject to the provisions of subsection (g) of this Section 4.1 and Section 8.2 hereof, if at any time after the Closing Date, the Company proposes to issue or sell Equity Securities ("Additional Securities") other than (i) under any Stock Option Plan, (ii) pursuant to the exercise of options under any Stock Option Plan, (iii) upon the exercise, exchange or conversion of any Convertible Securities, or (iv) for property other than money, the HOC Entities shall have the right to subscribe for and purchase Additional Securities, at the price at which such Additional Securities are offered for sale to other purchasers (the "Other Purchasers"), up to its Pro Rata Interest (as defined below) prior to giving effect to the issuance or sale of such Additional Securities. "Pro Rata Interest" means, at any relevant time, the ownership interest of HOC, expressed as a percentage, equal to: (i) the number of outstanding shares of common stock of the Company ("Common Stock") beneficially owned by the HOC Entities, including all shares of Common Stock issuable upon the conversion, exercise or exchange of all Convertible Securities beneficially owned by the HOC Entities divided by (ii) the aggregate number of outstanding shares of Common Stock, plus the number of shares of Common Stock issuable upon the conversion, exercise or exchange of all outstanding Convertible Securities;
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Subject to the provisions of subsection (g) of this Section 4.1 and Section 8.2 hereof, if at any time after the Closing Date, the Company proposes to issue or sell Equity Securities ("Additional Securities") other than (i) under any Stock Option Plan, (ii) pursuant to the exercise of options under any Stock Option Plan, (iii) upon the exercise, exchange or conversion of any Convertible Securities, or (iv) for property other than money, the HOC Entities may or may not have the right to subscribe for and purchase Additional Securities, at the price at which such Additional Securities are offered for sale to other purchasers (the "Other Purchasers"), up to its Pro Rata Interest (as defined below) prior to giving effect to the issuance or sale of such Additional Securities. "Pro Rata Interest" means, at any relevant time, the ownership interest of HOC, expressed as a fixed number of shares, equal to: (i) the number of outstanding shares of common stock of the Company ("Common Stock") beneficially owned by the HOC Entities, excluding shares of Common Stock issuable upon the conversion, exercise or exchange of all Convertible Securities beneficially owned by the HOC Entities divided by (ii) the aggregate number of outstanding shares of Common Stock, plus the number of shares of Common Stock issuable upon the conversion, exercise or exchange of all outstanding Convertible Securities;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall have the right' to 'may or may not have the right' makes the pre-emptive right uncertain, contradicting the intention of protecting HOC's investment. Defining Pro Rata Interest as a 'fixed number of shares' instead of a 'percentage' and exclusion from equation is directly contradictory. Now, it is unclear how the right works, which makes its enforcement unpredictable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Subject to the provisions of subsection (g) of this Section 4.1 and Section 8.2 hereof, if at any time after the Closing Date, the Company proposes to issue or sell Equity Securities ("Additional Securities") other than (i) under any Stock Option Plan, (ii) pursuant to the exercise of options under any Stock Option Plan, (iii) upon the exercise, exchange or conversion of any Convertible Securities, or (iv) for property other than money, the HOC Entities may or may not have the right to subscribe for and purchase Additional Securities, at the price at which such Additional Securities are offered for sale to other purchasers (the "Other Purchasers"), up to its Pro Rata Interest (as defined below) prior to giving effect to the issuance or sale of such Additional Securities. "Pro Rata Interest" means, at any relevant time, the ownership interest of HOC, expressed as a fixed number of shares, equal to: (i) the number of outstanding shares of common stock of the Company ("Common Stock") beneficially owned by the HOC Entities, excluding shares of Common Stock issuable upon the conversion, exercise or exchange of all Convertible Securities beneficially owned by the HOC Entities divided by (ii) the aggregate number of outstanding shares of Common Stock, plus the number of shares of Common Stock issuable upon the conversion, exercise or exchange of all outstanding Convertible Securities;
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces two major contradictions. First, it changes 'shall have the right' to 'may or may not have the right,' making the pre-emptive right uncertain, which directly conflicts with the original intent of protecting HOC's investment. This creates ambiguity where the original text provided certainty. Second, it redefines 'Pro Rata Interest' from a 'percentage' to a 'fixed number of shares' and alters the calculation by excluding shares issuable upon conversion, exercise, or exchange of Convertible Securities, making it incompatible with any clause expecting to use the initial defined meaning of Pro Rata Interest as a percentage to determine subscription rights.

perturbation.original_text: If HOC acquires the Additional Shares and subsequently acquires additional Shares such that it holds a Pro Rata Interest of 40% or more, HOC shall be entitled to appoint one (1) additional individual to the Board of Directors. For greater clarity, if HOC exercises the Option and holds a Pro Rata Interest of at least 40%, HOC shall be entitled to appoint a total of two (2) individuals to the Board of Directors. Upon receipt of a nomination from HOC for the second director and its satisfaction that the individual meets the qualification requirements for directors under applicable law, the Board of Directors agrees to expand its membership to five positions and appoint the individual nominated by HOC to the newly created vacancy. The Board of Directors further agrees that so long as HOC maintains a Pro Rata Interest of at least 35%, it shall nominate such individual to the slate of directors at each subsequent annual meeting so long as the provisions of Section 8.2 are not invoked. The Company further agrees that if HOC exercises the Option and so long as the provisions of Section 8.2 are not invoked, it will not take any action to authorize and will cause the Board of Directors not to authorize any proposal to expand the Board beyond five (5) members without the advance written approval of HOC.
perturbation.changed_text: If HOC acquires the Additional Shares and subsequently acquires additional Shares such that it holds a Pro Rata Interest of 40% or more, HOC may, at the company's discretion, be entitled to appoint one (1) additional individual to the Board of Directors. For greater clarity, if HOC exercises the Option and holds a Pro Rata Interest of at least 40%, HOC may, at the company's discretion, be entitled to appoint a total of two (2) individuals to the Board of Directors. Upon receipt of a nomination from HOC for the second director and its satisfaction that the individual meets the qualification requirements for directors under applicable law, the Board of Directors may or may not agrees to expand its membership to five positions and appoint the individual nominated by HOC to the newly created vacancy. The Board of Directors further agrees that so long as HOC maintains a Pro Rata Interest of at least 35%, it shall not nominate such individual to the slate of directors at each subsequent annual meeting so long as the provisions of Section 8.2 are not invoked. The Company further agrees that if HOC exercises the Option and so long as the provisions of Section 8.2 are not invoked, it will not take any action to authorize and will cause the Board of Directors not to authorize any proposal to expand the Board beyond five (5) members without the advance written approval of HOC.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'HOC shall be entitled' to 'HOC may, at the company's discretion, be entitled' removes the certainty of HOC's right to appoint directors, placing it under the company's control and contradicting the previous section 5.1, and now, is subject to company approval, which creates uncertainty. Moreover, changing 'Board of Directors agrees' to 'Board of Directors may or may not agrees' removes the certainty of the directors expanding membership, and now, it is not under the directors duty to expand its members. Changing 'it shall nominate' to 'it shall not nominate' contradicts the slate of directors, creating more uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If HOC acquires the Additional Shares and subsequently acquires additional Shares such that it holds a Pro Rata Interest of 40% or more, HOC may, at the company's discretion, be entitled to appoint one (1) additional individual to the Board of Directors. For greater clarity, if HOC exercises the Option and holds a Pro Rata Interest of at least 40%, HOC may, at the company's discretion, be entitled to appoint a total of two (2) individuals to the Board of Directors. Upon receipt of a nomination from HOC for the second director and its satisfaction that the individual meets the qualification requirements for directors under applicable law, the Board of Directors may or may not agrees to expand its membership to five positions and appoint the individual nominated by HOC to the newly created vacancy. The Board of Directors further agrees that so long as HOC maintains a Pro Rata Interest of at least 35%, it shall not nominate such individual to the slate of directors at each subsequent annual meeting so long as the provisions of Section 8.2 are not invoked. The Company further agrees that if HOC exercises the Option and so long as the provisions of Section 8.2 are not invoked, it will not take any action to authorize and will cause the Board of Directors not to authorize any proposal to expand the Board beyond five (5) members without the advance written approval of HOC.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty and company discretion where the original text guaranteed HOC's rights to appoint directors, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty around the Board of Director's actions. The original text states 'HOC shall be entitled' while the changed text says 'HOC may, at the company's discretion, be entitled'. This undermines the certainty of HOC's rights, contradicts the Board of Director's obligations and is a structural flaw that falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
